description_long: This object depicts two pigeons, each with a plump, rounded body and short necks, standing side by side. Their plumage is primarily a gradient of gray shades, with darker bands across the wings and tail, and lighter gray on the chest and underparts. The heads are small and rounded, with short, conical beaks and subtle facial markings. The legs are thin and pinkish, ending in three forward-facing toes and one backward-facing toe, typical of perching birds. The tails are short and squared, with visible dark barring. The overall appearance is stylized but clearly modeled after the common city pigeon, Columba livia.
description: A pair of plump, gray pigeons with dark wing bars, pink legs, and short, squared tails, standing together in a lifelike pose.
description_view_0: Two pigeons are shown side by side, one upright and the other pecking downward, highlighting their rounded bodies and pink legs.
description_view_1: The birds are viewed from the side, emphasizing the gradient gray plumage, dark wing bars, and the downward angle of one bird's head.
description_view_2: A rear view reveals the squared tails and the distinct dark barring across the wings and tail feathers.
description_view_3: A frontal view shows the rounded chests, small heads, and the subtle facial features of both pigeons.
attribute_plumage_pattern: gray with dark wing bars and tail bands, lighter underparts, subtle gradient on chest
